name: Forgewright Lite Performance Validation
description: Golden evaluation suite for Forgewright Lite performance validation using a cheap model

tasks.prompt: Fix the division function in calculator.py to return None when dividing by zero, instead of raising ZeroDivisionError. Do not change any other behavior.
verifierCommands: python3 -c "import hashlib, os, sys; (sys.exit(1) if not os.path.exists('MANIFEST.sha256') else None); [sys.exit(1) for line in open('MANIFEST.sha256') if line.strip() and not line.startswith('#') for expected, path in [line.strip().split(None, 1)] if not os.path.exists(path) or hashlib.sha256(open(path, 'rb').read()).hexdigest() != expected]; sys.exit(0)"
python3 test_calculator.py

tasks.prompt: Fix parser.js to catch any JSON parsing errors and return an empty object {} instead of throwing an exception. Valid JSON should still parse correctly.
verifierCommands: python3 -c "import hashlib, os, sys; (sys.exit(1) if not os.path.exists('MANIFEST.sha256') else None); [sys.exit(1) for line in open('MANIFEST.sha256') if line.strip() and not line.startswith('#') for expected, path in [line.strip().split(None, 1)] if not os.path.exists(path) or hashlib.sha256(open(path, 'rb').read()).hexdigest() != expected]; sys.exit(0)"
node test_parser.js

tasks.prompt: Implement a function get_file_hash(filepath) in hash_util.py that computes the SHA-256 hash of the file at the given filepath and returns it as a hexadecimal string. If the file does not exist, it must return None.

tasks.prompt: Create a python script check_compliance.py that reads a JSON file named policy.json and verifies that it complies with the following rules: 1) Must contain a 'version' key of type string; 2) Must contain an 'allowed_ips' key of type list.
